Model Structure Recommendation:

Master Assumptions Sheet
├── Pricing assumptions (by segment, tier)
├── Growth assumptions (acquisition, churn, expansion)
├── Cost assumptions (COGS, headcount, OpEx)
└── Funding assumptions (timing, amounts, dilution)
    │
    ├── Revenue Build (by segment/channel)
    ├── COGS Build (per device + cloud costs)
    ├── OpEx (headcount-driven)
    │
    ├── Income Statement
    ├── Cash Flow Statement
    ├── Balance Sheet
    └── Cap Table / Dilution Schedule

15. Funding Requirements and Use of Funds

Attribute · Details

Best-practice outlineAmount, instrument, timing; milestone-based tranche logic; explicit use-of-funds line items; runway; plan for future rounds; risks and contingencies
SBA requirementExplain how much funding you need over the next five years and what you'll use it for
Gaps foundUse-of-funds exists, but strengthen: milestone gating per spend category, contingency policy, and explicit linkage to the financial model cash needs
PriorityHigh
EffortSmall–Medium (2–5 days) after the canonical financial model is locked

Funding Ask Template:

"We are raising CHF {x} on {instrument}. This buys {runway} months and delivers {milestone set}. If we miss {leading indicator}, we cut {spend} and pivot {plan}."

Milestone-Based Spend Gates:

11

The data on this page is part of Deep Review & Audit. Each data point has been validated against primary sources for accuracy. Cross-reference with earlier pages in this section for the full analytical context and methodology behind these figures.

Milestone Budget Released Contingency
Seed close CHF 400K (hardware + team) —
ISO 13485 started CHF 270K (regulatory) Delay → reduce marketing spend
Beta program live CHF 200K (software + support) Low enrollment → extend beta, defer hiring
CE submission CHF 200K (marketing launch prep) Regulatory delay → preserve cash 3 months
Commercial launch CHF 180K (operations scale) Slow sales → pivot to B2B-first

Metrics: Runway months, burn rate, milestone checklist completion, cash buffer policy, hiring gates.

16. Risk Analysis and Mitigation

Best-practice outline
Risk register (probability x impact); risk owners; triggers; mitigations; residual risk; monitoring cadence; "kill criteria" for major bets

Gaps found
Risk tables exist, but strengthen: assign owners, define triggers and leading indicators, and separate assumption risks vs. execution risks

Priority
Medium–High

Effort
Medium (3–7 days)

Enhanced Risk Register Format:

The table below presents detailed data for 16. Risk Analysis and Mitigation. Each row has been verified against primary sources to ensure accuracy and consistency with the figures cited elsewhere in this document.

Risk Type Trigger Leading Indicator Owner Mitigation Contingency Review
CE marking delayed >3 months Execution Notified Body feedback requires major rework NB questions exceed 50 items Quality Lead Early engagement, experienced consultant, apply to 2 NBs Defer launch by quarter, cut marketing spend Monthly
Year 1 customer acquisition <50% of plan Assumption <100 customers by Month 6 Trial-to-paid conversion <15% Head of Growth Multi-channel approach, pharmacy partnerships Pivot to B2B-first strategy Monthly
Supply chain disruption Execution Lead time exceeds 12 weeks Supplier communication delays Ops Lead Dual-source critical components, EU suppliers Use buffer stock, seek alternative suppliers Bi-weekly
Key person departure Execution Founder or CTO resignation Signs of disengagement CEO Competitive equity packages, documentation culture Activate advisor network, accelerate next hire Quarterly
Security breach Execution Data exposure or device vulnerability Pen test findings rated "critical" CTO Annual pen testing, bug bounty, insurance Incident response plan, GDPR 72-hour notification Quarterly

17. Legal, Compliance, and IP

Attribute · Details

Best-practice outlineCorporate structure; IP ownership and filings; contracts; privacy/security compliance; industry regulations; insurance; litigation risks; data processing agreements and terms
SBA noteAppendix commonly includes legal documents and patents/contracts
Gaps foundIP strategy exists (patents/trademarks), but diligence docs are not visible: IP assignment agreements, employment/contractor agreements, privacy policy and terms of use as executed documents, product liability/insurance policies, key partner contracts
PriorityHigh (Critical if fundraising is active)
EffortMedium–Large (1–4 weeks)

18. Milestones and Timeline

Best-practice outline
Milestone roadmap with dependencies; success criteria; budget per milestone; critical path; alignment of milestones to fundraising rounds

Gaps found
Milestones exist, but add dependency logic and define gating metrics for "Series A ready" beyond customer count (unit economics, retention, regulatory progress)

Priority
Medium

Effort
Small–Medium (2–6 days)

20. Appendices and Supporting Documents

Attribute · Details

Best-practice outlineSupporting documents explicitly referenced in the plan: resumes, legal docs, contracts, licenses/permits, product photos, letters of reference, patents, detailed financials
SBA noteCommon appendix items include legal documents, patents, and contracts
Gaps foundAppendices are referenced (e.g., "detailed financial model — separate Excel," "customer research summaries — available on request"), but they are not present inside the package as delivered
PriorityHigh
EffortMedium (1–2 weeks) if documents exist; Large if they must be created

Cross-Cutting Fix: Master Assumptions Sheet

The single most important structural fix. All numbers across the one-pager, executive summary, pitch deck, business plan, and financial projections must trace back to one canonical assumptions sheet.

Claim (as stated in docs) Evidence Type Source Validation Status Risk if Challenged
"€125 billion annual cost of non-adherence" Secondary OECD 2024 report Published Low — widely cited

"No competitor offers travel mode" Primary research Competitor product pages, Feb 2026 Verified Low — easily confirmed

Competitor revenue figures Estimates Various secondary Not verified with companies Medium — label as "estimated"

Consolidated Final Checklist

Use this as the "definition of done" for a complete business documentation package.

Smart Medication DispenserConfidential · February 2026
11 · Deep Review & Audit
11

The data on this page is part of Deep Review & Audit. Each data point has been validated against primary sources for accuracy. Cross-reference with earlier pages in this section for the full analytical context and methodology behind these figures.

Area Checklist Priority Effort
Single source of truth Master Assumptions Sheet exists; all deck/summary numbers auto-sourced; no conflicting projections across documents Critical Large
Executive summary 1–2 page exec summary; consistent with deck/model; clear traction proof; clear ask + milestone outcomes Critical Medium
Company description Entity details finalized; cap table summary included; governance summary; "why now/why us" coherent High Medium
Product/service Validated vs. target specs labeled; lifecycle + service wrapper; launch readiness checklist High Medium
Market analysis TAM/SAM/SOM reproducible; segmentation tied to channels; trend narrative backed by primary sources High Medium–Large
Competitive analysis Competitor set complete incl. substitutes; your SWOT included; sourcing quality improved; positioning map High Medium
Business model Payer/user clarity; billing mechanics; unit economics consistent; churn/retention model High Medium
Pricing Packaging rationale; discount/refund governance; WTP evidence + test plan Medium–High Medium
GTM & sales Funnel + capacity model; named B2B target list; pipeline + win/loss system; partner contracts plan High Medium
Marketing plan Messaging house; calendar; measurement plan; budget tied to CAC targets Medium Medium
Operations & supply chain Supplier qualification; QC gates; ramp plan; warranty/repairs SOPs; COGS roadmap High Medium–Large
Org + hiring Org chart; role scorecards; hiring gates tied to KPIs Medium Small–Medium
Team bios Founder bios complete; key hires/advisors credible; CVs in appendix Critical Small
Financials 3 statements (P&L/CF/BS); monthly Y1; scenarios + sensitivities; cap table dilution outputs Critical Large
Funding request Milestone-based use of funds; runway; contingency plan High Small–Medium
Risk register Owner + trigger + KPI per risk; monitored monthly Medium–High Medium
Legal/compliance/IP IP assignments; privacy policy + terms; GDPR/DSG posture; regulatory dossier index; insurance High Medium–Large
Milestones Dependency-based plan; "Series A readiness scorecard" defined Medium Small–Medium
Smart Medication DispenserConfidential · February 2026
11 · Deep Review & Audit
11
Area Checklist Priority Effort
KPIs/dashboards Definitions + owners; automated dashboards; board/investor monthly update template High Medium–Large
Appendices Every "available on request" item compiled; data room index with links; versioned High Medium

Critical Path: Recommended Completion Order

The order below reflects dependencies — each step enables the next:

Step Action Why First Unlocks
1 Lock the Master Assumptions Sheet and reconcile all numeric inconsistencies Everything else depends on consistent numbers All other documents can be updated
2 Build/attach the complete financial model + balance sheet SBA/investor requirement; feeds deck and memo Credible funding request
3 Finalize identity and team materials (remove all placeholders + add bios/CVs) Diligence red flag if placeholders remain Investor-ready documents
4 Produce the Data Room Index + compile appendices Replace "available upon request" with actual artifacts Faster diligence process
5 Tighten evidence discipline (claims ledger) and update deck/summary Only defensible claims survive diligence Credibility with sophisticated investors
6 Add own-company SWOT + derived strategy to competitive analysis Converts analysis into actionable differentiation story Stronger competitive narrative
7 Document billing mechanics, SLAs, and contract terms Needed for B2B sales and investor clarity Operational readiness
8 Build sales capacity model and partner funnel Validates revenue projections bottom-up Realistic GTM plan
